title: Förvaltaren varnar för USA – ser köpläge i Europa
summary_md:
# Förvaltaren varnar för USA – ser köpläge i Europa

**Feed**: dagen-med-di  
**Published**: 2026-06-22  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

Two fund managers (Lasse and Ulf) argue the US market is broadly overvalued, driven by an AI/data-center investment boom and mega-IPOs like SpaceX, but warn the rally is sustained only by still-low rates and will eventually break when rates rise. They note a rotation away from capital-light Mag7 software names toward capital-intensive hardware/chip makers, and flag that Nvidia's ~75% margins should normalize as competition arrives. They see better value in Europe/Sweden, which starts from lower rates and a weaker cycle with fresh fiscal/infrastructure spending.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟠 NVDA — Nvidia | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Gross margin at 75% vs historic ~40%; bulk of rally is price adjustment not volume; new chip competition emerging.

#### Reasoning

Managers say Nvidia is 'dancing near the door' — most of the surge reflects a price re-rating rather than sustainable volume growth, since there aren't enough fabs to grow volume 100%/yr. Margins at 75% vs a normal ~40% imply mean-reversion once new competitors ship chips. They advise riding the AI trend but trimming along the way rather than adding aggressively.

#### Sell Triggers

- if new competitor chips erode gross margins toward 40%
- if interest rates rise enough to end the AI boom
- if data-center orders fail to convert into realized returns

> *"Tittar man på Nvidia så brukar man ligga på en bruttomarginal på 40%. Det kanske är det man ska tjäna. Och nu ligger man på 75%. Marknadsekonomin, då ska det komma ny konkurrens."*

---

### 🟢 MU — Micron | **BUY** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Rally leadership rotating from capital-light Mag7 software to capital-intensive chip/hardware makers like Micron.

#### Reasoning

Lasse wants to see a shift away from the Mag7 toward hardware manufacturers, noting the upside has already moved to makers like Micron and Broadcom amid the memory/processor demand surge. The view is constructive on hardware as the next leg of the AI boom, though tempered by warnings that pricing will eventually adjust as supply catches up.

#### Sell Triggers

- if memory/chip prices normalize as new fab capacity comes online
- if the AI capex boom stalls
- if rates rise and end the boom

> *"Uppgången har ju egentligen flyttats till mer tillverkande bolag som Micron, Broadcom och alla de här."*

---
